---
title: "UKB chr1 Dense Store — bcftools Build Time Benchmark"
subtitle: "chr1 × 100 UKB analyses · bcftools vs cyvcf2 VCF reader comparison"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
---

## Summary

bcftools is **2.26× faster** than cyvcf2 for building the UKB chr1
dense store (388.5s vs 876.63s).
Query performance is unchanged (queries do not use the VCF reader).

## Build time comparison

| Reader | Build time | vs other |
|---|---:|---:|
| cyvcf2 (previous) | 876.63s | 1.00× (baseline) |
| bcftools (current) | 388.5s | 2.26× faster |

Dataset: 762,080 variants × 100 analyses,
290 MB store, 0 liftover failures.

## Query timings (confirmation run)

Queries do not use the VCF reader. Timings confirm no regression from the reader change.

| Query | Median ms | p95 ms | Result count | vs besdq |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| regional | 97.6 | 130.4 | 324,048 | 19.6× ⚠ |
| phewas | 2.6 | 3.0 | 100 | 2.9× ⚠ |
| tophits | 1.3 | 1.4 | 14,557 | 1.9× |
| bulk | 1764.2 | 2802.6 | 762,080 | 3.6× ⚠ |
| random_lookup | 224.2 | 298.4 | 598 | 7.4× ⚠ |

## Interpretation

The bcftools reader replaces cyvcf2 across all three VCF functions
(`stream_vcf_variants`, `stream_vcf_associations`, `read_vcf_study_type`).
The build speedup is consistent with the known overhead of Python-level
cyvcf2 iteration vs bcftools C-level record processing.

Remaining query gaps vs besdq are due to zarr chunk decompression overhead
(100-chunk reads per column for bulk; oindex reads across 3,241 rows for regional),
not the VCF reader. The top-hits pattern is now within the 2× target (1.89×).

## Provenance

- JSON: `opengwasdb_vcf_ukb_chr1_bcftools_benchmark.json`
- Array API benchmark: `opengwasdb_vcf_ukb_chr1_array_benchmark.json`
- Store path: `docs/benchmark-output/vcf_ukb_chr1_dense.opengwasdb`
- ADR: `docs/adr/0019-two-pass-vcf-build-with-inline-liftover.md`
